content.problemStatement:
The kingdom of Treeland consists of NNN cities (numbered 111 through NNN) connected by N−1N-1N−1 bidirectional roads in such a way that there is a path between each pair of cities.

In order to increase security in Treeland, the government decided to set up police offices in KKK of its cities. For each valid iii, the iii-th office is in city ViV_iVi​ and it has a radius of efficiency RiR_iRi​.

Let's define the security level SiS_iSi​ of each city iii as follows:


Initially, the security level of each city is equal to zero.
Then, for each police office jjj that was built (1≤j≤K1 \le j \le K1≤j≤K), the security levels change in the following manner:

The security level of the city VjV_jVj​ increases by RjR_jRj​.
The security levels of all cities at the distance 111 from VjV_jVj​ increase by Rj−1R_j-1Rj​−1.
...
The security levels of all cities at the distance Rj−1R_j-1Rj​−1 from VjV_jVj​ increase by 111.


Formally, for each city iii, the jjj-th office increases the security level of this city by max(0,Rj−distance(i,Vj))\mathrm{max}(0, R_j - \mathrm{distance}(i, V_j))max(0,Rj​−distance(i,Vj​)).
Alice was in charge of calculating the new security levels of all cities after the offices were built. She already finished her job and noticed an interesting coincidence: for each valid iii, SVi=RiS_{V_i} = R_iSVi​​=Ri​ holds. Now she has challenged you to find the security levels of all cities.
constraints: 1≤Vi≤N1 \le V_i \le N1≤Vi​≤N for each valid iii
V1,V2,…,VKV_1, V_2, \ldots, V_KV1​,V2​,…,VK​ are pairwise distinct
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 8⋅1058 \cdot 10^58⋅105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
Then, N−1N-1N−1 lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting that cities uuu and vvv are connected by a road.
KKK more lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers ViV_iVi​ and RiR_iRi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers S1,S2,…,SNS_1, S_2, \ldots, S_NS1​,S2​,…,SN​.
